fields.description: Automated: fields passing every deterministic check AND the verify-model pass are auto-approved. Manual: everything lands Pending for human review. Callers may override per run.
fields.label: Default Mode

fields.description: What this action does — shown to humans and to the orchestrator model.
fields.label: Purpose

fields.description: The task prompt sent to the executor model. Edit freely — no code change needed.
fields.label: Instructions

fields.description: Domain knowledge injected into the prompt (formats, business facts, edge cases).
fields.label: Knowledge

fields.description: Plain-English verification rules the verify model applies when comparing the extraction against the source.
fields.label: Verification Rules

fields.description: Static request refinement used when routing is deterministic (single-model shortcut) and no orchestrator call runs.
fields.label: Request Notes

fields.description: Opt-in: let the orchestrator author request notes even when routing is forced. Off = prompt content never changes implicitly with the model roster.
fields.label: Use LLM Request Notes

fields.description: Repair missing/colliding bounding boxes by OCR word-box candidate SELECTION (deterministic text match first, numbered-marks model pick second) instead of asking the model for raw coordinates. Needs the `tesseract` binary on the server; silently falls back to free-form repair without it.
fields.label: OCR-Anchored Bbox Repair

fields.description: After any bbox repair, crop the claimed box and have the verifier model confirm the value is actually printed inside it. Unconfirmed boxes are removed (models hallucinate boxes rather than abstain).
fields.label: Crop-Back Check on Repaired Boxes

fields.description: Attach each claimed region's crop to the verify pass so the verifier reads the exact pixels behind every claim instead of re-scanning the whole document from memory.
fields.label: Per-Claim Crops in Verify

fields.description: Skip the LLM second-opinion re-read of the document. Write safety rests on the deterministic corroboration gate, not this pass. Cuts token cost for a trusted single-pass extractor.
fields.label: Skip Model Verify

fields.description: The resolving agent runs on text only (no image); it queries the vision model via the built-in ask_document tool when it needs to re-read the document. Cheaper matching loop.
fields.label: Agent Text-Only (brain)

fields.description: Resolve by exact-key proof in code (auto-apply only gate-passing unique matches); use the LLM only to extract, never to freely search/decide. Cheapest + no matching hallucination.
fields.label: Deterministic First (proof-based matching)

fields.description: When deterministic_first can't resolve a reference, fall back to the LLM tool-calling brain instead of leaving it for review. Off = unresolved go straight to human review (cheaper).
fields.label: Agent Fallback When Unresolved

fields.description: After extraction, snap each boxed value to its OCR word geometry when the text is found at exactly one confident location near the claimed box (tighten-only; never moves or fills boxes).
fields.label: Snap Boxes to OCR Words

fields.description: JSON list of fields. Per field: key, label, kind (scalar|array), required, format (regex:… | date:indian-ddmmyyyy | amount | digits:N | strip-spaces), bbox_required, cross_check {doctype, field, filters_template}.
fields.label: Output Schema (JSON)
Record Matching (optional — reconcile against ERP records)

fields.description: JSON. When set, after extraction the engine fetches candidate records and the model picks which one(s) the document refers to. Keys: target_doctype; candidate_query {filters (dict or [[field,op,value]]), or_filters, fields, limit, order_by} — values may template {schema_key} (first extracted value) or {context.key}; match_guidance (plain-English matching rules for the model); confidence_threshold (0..1, below = doubt → review). Leave blank to disable matching.
fields.label: Match Config (JSON)
Tools (agentic execution — the engine calls these to resolve the document itself)

fields.description: JSON list of tools the engine may call (function-calling). Each: name; description (the model reads this to decide when to call it); method (a whitelisted server method — a Frappe built-in like frappe.client.get_list, or one your app writes); kind ("read" | "write"); defaults (fixed kwargs merged under the model's args); parameters (JSON-schema of the model-supplied args); permission_doctype (write tools are permission-checked against it). The model only ever picks a tool by name — the method is fixed here. Write tools receive the run's reference as trusted _context. Leave blank to use match_config / no tools.
fields.label: Tools (JSON)

fields.description: Domain guidance appended to the agentic resolution prompt — HOW to decide (e.g. each reference maps to its own record; prefer the most recent match).
fields.label: Agent Instructions

fields.description: Max verify→repair rounds per run.
fields.label: Max Rounds

fields.description: Hard cap on LLM calls per run — checked before every call.
fields.label: Max Calls Per Run

fields.description: Wall-clock budget per run in seconds. Exceeded → stop repairing, record remaining deficiencies.
fields.label: Run Seconds Budget
